Basically in CAP Node.js, you can now create ECMAScript module projects with cds add esm command. For CAP Java, simpler recursive hierarchies can be realized with aggregate aggregation dot recursive hierarchies annotation. You can now filter and sort by sub-elements of cds.map elements in CAP Java. Enhanced instance-based authorization is supported in CAP Java with introduction of a new configuration option, reject-selected-unauthorized-entity. Last update is about MTX. In June release of Caphire,it was announced that only few annotations are allowed as extensions. You can now add exceptions for these restrictions, but at your own risk, using the annotations property. Please visit the Capphire documentation for further details. You can find the link to the documentation in the description.

[DJ] You probably know that SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA have been retired to be replaced by SAP Business Process Automation. Now you're going to have to do some migration and recently there have been some migration webinars to help you along the way. In Lujain's blog post there are also some resources listed that can support you in this migration and coming soon to this blog post will be the addition of links to these migration webinar replays in case you missed them. So check out Lujain's blog post and happy migrating!

[Thomas] We'd like to point your attention to a recent blog post by Marian Zeis that covers the topic of SAP BTP authentication but in the context of pipelines. He talks about how in automated pipelines can also be often be difficult to handle authentication mechanisms securely and simply and he offers an approach using the SAP Passport instead of certificate based authentication and using the Playwright module to simplify the processing of the passports. He does a really good job of describing the overall details of this solution, some code samples of how to set up the context with the certificate, and how to use the Playwright client certificate login package. He even has links to the GitHub repos and the package authentication, the package details, as as well as a code sample from his own GitHub repository on how to set up and use the scenario that he's describing. So this is a challenge that you face. We think you should definitely check out his blog post and see how he has solved the solution for himself and shared the solution with the community.

entry.title: Santa builds process to distribute Christmas presents
entry.content:
No one would deny that Santa has a complex process to give out presents for Christmas. 

But several key new SAP Build Process Automation features has enabled Santa to automate the process using a true Fusion Team of reindeer, elves, and his wife. Among the features:

  • Scheduled triggers
  • Script task
  • Custom variables
  • Subprocesses
  • Go to task

And the process can actually teach us some of the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation.

Scheduled Triggers

Probably the most obvious feature to help Santa is the scheduled trigger for regular processes. We could rely on one of the elves to send an API call, but the scheduled trigger is much more reliable.

Action and Data Type to Get Children 

The process uses an action to retrieves a list of children.

We first created a data type to represent a child (name string and a Boolean on whether they were nice or naughty).

Then we created custom variables, one to hold the full list of children, and another to hold the current child in our iteration.

Then we run an action to get the children.

Script Task and GoTo for Iterating Children

Now we create a script task to move the last child into the current child variable, and to remove that child from the full list.

And finally we create an iteration sequence by a Go To back to the start, where we have a condition to see if the list has more children, and 

Subprocesses

We create subprocesses like "Prepare Reindeer and Sled" since we are likely to need to do that during the year, and we create subprocesses like "Deliver via Chimney" in order to simplify and isolate our logic.

Obvious, in a more realistic case we would have created additional logic to review all the actions of a child to determine if they were nice or naughty, as Santa "sees you when you're sleepin'" and "knows when you're awake", and likely is aware of all actions of a children, so we might use a Decision to make that determination.

And we might create an automation to deal with the complexity of building the toys, handling emails in Outlook from kids wanting presents, and looking for sales on toys on the web. 

🎅🎄❄🤶⛪🕎🕌🤗🦌

Hoping the holiday season and the new year brings you peace and love and happiness, and everything you dream about.

entry.title: Wrapping up 2024 for Application Development, Automation and Integration on SAP BTP: What a year!
entry.content:
2024 has been an incredibly busy year for our application development, automation and integration portfolio on the SAP Business Technology Platform. As the year draws to a close, I wanted to take some time to reflect on our achievements. Indeed, I am very grateful to the people in my teams who have worked very hard to deliver so many improvements to our solutions, and I would like to say a big thank you to all of them.

Of course, you've heard a lot about Joule, our AI co-pilot, over the course of the year. In fact, we have been integrating AI in general, and Generative AI with Joule in particular, into all components of our portfolio, as we announced earlier. And we demonstrated our AI capabilities at SAP TechEd two months ago. This deployment of Business AI has generated great interest from our customers and partners, as well as analysts.

But we've also introduced many new features and enhancements, which I'm proud to present in detail below.

SAP Build

In terms of application development and automation, in addition to embedding AI in all of our solutions, our biggest improvement in 2024 was to enrich SAP Build with pro-code capabilities. Indeed, we announced SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd 2023, made available in March this year, and we recently added ABAP Environments to SAP Build. SAP Build now allows business users and developers to work across their low-code, Java/JavaScript and ABAP projects from a unique starting point, the SAP Build lobby, benefitting of seamless interoperability capabilities fostering fusion development.

With this, SAP Build is now THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development. And SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions with SAP Build directly from the ERP business application, thanks to our new Extensibility Wizard for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This year, we have worked hard to improve the integration of the SAP Build components, both with other components of SAP BTP and with other SAP solutions: for example, SAP Build Process Automation is now integrated with SAP Build Apps and also with Advanced Event Mesh, applications created with SAP Build Apps can be deployed in SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Work Zone supports multi-tenancy.

Finally, I would like to mention that we have improved the usability of SAP Build, for example with a new process layout and script task editor in SAP Build Process Automation and guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone.

This image summarizes all the improvements we delivered this year:

A few weeks ago, Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP). I would like to thank the SAP Build team for achieving this great result!

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – November 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – July 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q2 2024 Edition
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Seamless Integration of Processes and Apps
  • What’s New with SAP Build Process Automation: January 2024

SAP Integration Suite

In 2024 we have also improved SAP Integration Suite in many different areas:

Regarding SAP PI/PO migration, which is a topic on which I published a blog post earlier this year to explain Advanced Event Mesh and Edge Integration Cell, we introduced a Pipeline concept and new rules added for migration assessment.

Edge Integration Cell is now available on Google Cloud and Red Hat OpenShift. It supports outgoing connections through a HTTP proxy and provides offline availability during periods of temporary connectivity loss.

This year, Event Mesh became a capability of SAP Integration Suite, which also allows direct consumption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Events and distributed tracing, from sending to receiving applications across one or multiple event brokers.

We have been investing heavily in improving developer efficiency and operational excellence. A few of Gen AI Increments that we recently launched were to accelerate development of integration flows with Gen AI and analyze APIs using API Anomaly detection in API Management

You can also accelerate connectivity with SAP Integration Suite using new adapters for Coupa, NetSuiteERP, SnowFlake, and Amazon DynamoDB, SMB, Jira, Amazon EventBridge, HubSpot, Anaplan with many more planned to be released soon.

We enhanced Cloud Integration and API Management user experience with SAP Horizon theme. Enriched the Cloud Integration capability with the ability to inspect content size for all the integration artifacts, manage access at the package level, validate integration flow design as per the guidelines in integration flow editor.

Furthermore, API Management now supports multiple target endpoints in API proxy tooling and recently launched Developer Hub for search, discovery and consumption of APIs. We also launched oData provisioning as a new capability.

We have onboarded 25+ partners with a contract and further enriched our marketplace with 53+ new partner content in particular 7 new APIs, 4 Processes, 20+ Integration packages and 22+ Validated partner use cases added on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Finally, B2B Scenarios for Trading Partner Management (TPM) and Integration Advisor have also been enriched with functionalities like archiving of Payload data for Trading Partner Management scenarios, EDI flat file payloads can be directly used to simulate mappings, for easier access to envelope fields these can be added in the Integration Advisor, simplified mass-maintenance in TPM, support for custom SOA messages in Integration Advisor, new Tradacoms message standard.

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Whats New For SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – June & July 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – Spring 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – February 2024
  • What's New for SAP Integration Suite - January 2024

Feel free to follow our #WhatsNewInSAPIntegrationSuite on SAP Community and register for our webinar for updates on upcoming innovations!

Finally, I’m also proud to share that with SAP Integration Suite SAP has been named a Leader for 4th consecutive time by Gartner® in its 2024 Magic Quadrant™ for iPaaS. In addition, SAP is a Leader in IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Business-to-Business Middleware 2024 Vendor Assessment.

Kudos to the team for these achievements!

What’s for 2025?

As you’ve seen above, we delivered a lot of new features and enhancements in 2024. And for sure we will continue in 2025!

You can find out what we have planned for next year on our public roadmap for application development, automation and integration on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

With that, I wish you a Merry Christmas and very happy holidays!  🎅🏻🎄

entry.title: Posting attachments to ECC from SAP BTP Cloud Foundry workflow [Build process automation]
entry.content:
Hi All,

We had a requirement to post attachments with different extensions to backend [ECC]. Required functionality was to fetch file content for respective posted invoice from azure, which can be one or multiple files with different extensions, convert it in acceptable ECC format and post it to ECC.

When I was implementing this functionality, I couldn’t find any straightforward blog which would help me explain how to send attachments from workflow to ECC or any other backend system. Hence, I explored some other options, made it successful and thought I should share so that it can be useful and can be referred by others who want to achieve similar kind of functionality in their projects. Hope this helps.

We had a requirement where we wanted to send attachments to ECC. This requirement was to post attachments to backend only if invoice posting is successful.

Here our complete logic flow is as below:

  • User will raise invoice request where user has flexibility to add documents with any extension.
  • Request will be stored in azure and BTP workflow will be triggered for the same for posting the invoice to backend.
  • Once invoice posting is successful, workflow will fetch the attachment file content, and as is send to ECC with required parameters against related invoice.

 

Steps to add logic flow for posting attachments to ECC.

  • First, I am checking if posting of invoice is successful, as we want to send attachments only for success scenario and not for failures

 

 

 

  • If it’s successful, fetch the files from invoice request, one invoice can have multiple files with different extensions.

 

 

 

 

  • Fetch the file content from azure by sending file name in required payload format to API call. Collect the response from azure in response variable for next step.

 

 

 

 

 

  • Prepare payload for posting document to ECC. Header parameters and payload content is what we ll require to send to entity set.

 

 

 

 

 

Header parameters as above.

And file content we will send as is from azure response variable to “Request variable” section to avoid any corruption or additional quotes which gets added due to reassignment.

 

  • Once posting step is completed, we will handle the notification for success/error. And in next step we will check if files are remaining to post.

 

  • Then, we’ll check flag [which we can set by shifting or removing files form original file array of one or multiple files] for remaining documents, as we are posting one document at a time. Once all files are posted, we will complete the workflow.

 

 

 

  • From backend we must convert the file content [which is base64] in X-string to process it further. 

Once all files are posted in backend, we can view all posted attachments against invoice as below.

 

 

Using this approach, you can post files easily to backend, as well you can view attached files from backend.

 

 

That is all folks, this is how I achieved the posting files to backend. You can modify the flow and steps as per your own requirement. Good Luck!

entry.title: How to Use Formulas in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
In SAP Build Apps, transformation formulas play a vital role in dynamically manipulating data within applications. Like Excel formulas, these predefined functions and expressions allow you to build logic seamlessly without the need for custom coding. By leveraging transformation formulas, you can efficiently transform data for UI bindings, API responses, and other app logic requirements.

Formulas serve as one of the primary component binding types in SAP Build Apps. This means they can be applied in almost any part of the application to bind and manipulate data dynamically. For instance, when binding a "Text" or "Dropdown" component value, you can simply select the binding icon in the Properties tab and choose "Formula" as the binding type. This flexibility extends to tasks such as validating data fetched from an API, validating the data, Changing the Formats of display data, String and Arithmetic functions, etc., Using formulas, you can access all existing variables in the application that can then be modified.

We developed a prototype for creating a Business Partner using SAP Build Apps integrated with SAP Build Process Automation. Here, SAP On-Prem systems are connected to read data from the Business Partner API and display it as Value Helps for dropdown values

 

 

Here’s an overview of common transformation formulas that we used in SAP Build Apps for reading the data from backend and displaying as value helps:

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Build Apps provides an extensive range of built-in functions to simplify transformations, covering categories like:

  • Text Operations: To Check if a value exists, Select Query , or match patterns using regex.
  • Date Functions: Manipulate and format dates dynamically to the local format.
  • Color and Object Handling: Dynamically update colors based on conditions, objects, and other data types.
  • Arithmetic Operations: Perform calculations and computations directly such as sum, absolute values, is_even, is_odd, divide, multiply etc,.

 

In addition to built-in formulas, you can design custom transformation formulas to handle specific functionalities within your app. Here are a few common use cases:To map an input field of Dropdown type, we can map the data that needs to read from a datasource

  • Mapping Data to a Dropdown Input Field -  To bind a dropdown component to a field in data source as a value help,we can map the desired fields as:

MAP(data.ZCDV_BusinessPartnerRole1, {label:item.BPRoleText,value:item.BusinessPartnerRole})

 

 

  • Displaying Unique Fields Without Duplicates - To ensure a dropdown contains only unique values and no duplicates with different combinations

UNIQUE_BY_KEY(MAP(data.ZCDV_BupaCountry1,{label:item.CountryText, value:item.Country}), "value")

  • Fetching State/Region Data Based on Selected Country input - To dynamically filter and display regions or states in dropdown based on the selected country

 

MAP(SELECT(data.ZCDV_BupaCountry1, item.Country == pageVars.bp_inputs.country), {label: item.RegionText, value: item.RegionName})

 

  • Checking if Input is Empty - To validate whether an input field is blank or contains a value for conditional processing

IS_EMPTY(pageVars.bp_inputs.country)

 

  • Text transformations– Converting Variables to Uppercase/Lowercase using text transformations.Also, we can validate pattern for email using IS_EMAIL or REGEX functions.

UPPERCASE(data.A_BusinessPartner1[0].Initials)

Note: In SAP Build Apps, custom logic or formulas cannot be directly written for all data sources. Only APIs that are exposed can be utilized for any kind of data transformation or manipulation.

I hope this blog post provides you with a understanding of formulas in SAP Build Apps and how they can be leveraged to enhance data manipulation within your applications.

entry.title: MDG Cloud Ready Mode: Custom Workflow template in SAP build process automation with S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi All,

Season's Greetings!!

As we enter 2025, technology space is evolving at a rapid pace with GenAI, Agentic LLM's findings it's way in each and every enterprise level application. With Joule taking a bigger role in all SAP cloud applications, MDG has also been integrated with Joule for cloud ready mode with S4HANA 2023 FPS02. Markus discusses this in his blog Key Presentations about SAP Master Data Governance... - SAP Community.

In the blog MDG Cloud ready Mode: Integration of S/4HANA with SAP Build process automation & SAP Build work zone I talked about step-by-step details on how to set up the integration between S/4HANA on-prem to SAP BTP build process automation. In this blog we are going to explore how to set up our own workflow template in build process automation and link it with MDG process template.

INTRODUCTION:

In any MDG implementation, a big part of the design phase is to understand the workflow requirements and create custom workflows in BRF+ to cater to these requirements. In the MDG cloud ready mode also SAP has provided option to create your own Workflow templates. This has to be done in SAP build process automation.

There are however some limitations in terms of the current capabilities. A simple example is currently the control once transferred to MDC process model, can't be sent back to build process automation workflow. 

The way in which i have setup the custom workflow is little different from what is documented in the SAP help page. You can find it here.Custom Workflow and Visibility (Optional) | SAP Help Portal.

Essentially, I have not taken the standard Workflow template application in Build process automation as base, instead I have created completely different workflow.

PS: I followed the tutorial Create an API Trigger for the Process | SAP Tutorials to create the Build process automation project, so you can refer to this tutorial for step-by-step details.

MAIN CONTENT:

1. Create a blank WF project in build process automation. This workflow will be a trigger-based workflow, where trigger will be provided by the MDG process for cloud ready mode. 

2. For a trigger-based API, we need to define the input data structure of the API which is a data type. MasterDataProcess is the data type which was created with multiple fields.

3.  We have to create the process input to the API trigger; in this case I have kept only the processID as the mandatory input for the trigger.

4. Now we need to create the process artifact, which will take the Approve or Reject decision. In this case again it's only approval, rejection scenario can also be built on the build process automation side. Approve1 is the decision form 

 

 

The add quoates step, is technical step as the payload to S4HANA need to contain quotes for the WF ID instance. Currently its going without quotes, this might be overcome with future releases.

4. Once this is completed, we need to invoke a particular service SWF_CPWF_NOTIFICATION_SRV which will call S/4HANA and send the Workflow decision and ensure the process continues further. 

This service needs to be added as a dependency to your build process automation project.

This service expects SAP client and the workflow instanceID as input.

5. Once all these steps are completed the build process automation project along with the API triggers need to be deployed.

6. From the monitoring tab we can locate our custom workflow which is deployed and copy the ID.

7. Create a custom process template and in step workflow column paste the ID from step6.

Once this step is completed, the integration between the custom workflow template and the S4HANA MDG cloud ready mode process template is completed.

9. Below are the 2 methods which are useful for troubleshooting any integration issues with your custom workflow.


CONCLUSION:

This blog outlines few of the important steps, needed to deploy a custom workflow template in Build process automation and integrate it with MDG cloud ready mode process template on S4HANA on-prem.

MDG cloud ready mode is the next gen deployment option for Master data governance following the cloud first approach of SAP.

Hope you this blog provides some guidance during your MDG cloud ready mode implementation.

Please share your thoughts and questions if any.

Regards

Anirban

SAP Master Data Governance   SAP Build Process Automation   SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition   SAP S/4HANA   SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition SAP Business Technology Platform   ABAP Cloud   SAP S/4HANA business partner

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Build Faster, Smarter, and Efficiently

Welcome to the Q4 2024 edition of our "What's New "blog series. SAP Build is a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This quarter, we're excited to highlight many new releases, including AI-driven features that transform your ability to automate and innovate. Let's dive in!

Below, you will find product-specific highlights for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation

Create and Manage Process Variants

SAP Build Process Automation now allows you to create and manage process variants. With pre-defined process steps and guardrails, process experts and professional developers can model different variants for different user groups using a configuration-only experience. By defining variant conditions and actions, workflows can be adapted to your specific business needs. With this feature, you can manage tailored workflows for various scenarios without impacting the overall process template. Learn how to create and manage process variants in the SAP Help Portal.

Consume RFC / BAPI in a Process via Actions

To help you extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs. Utilizing the BAPI node library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. Easily search and browse RFC actions to locate the necessary functions for your workflows, maintain system integrity, and reduce complexity.

 

SAP Build Code

Generative AI Capabilities in the Mobile Development Kit

Enhanced generative AI capabilities for the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) can now help you simplify your app creation process. Developers can:

  • Generate Application Logic: Automatically produce metadata-driven logic, reducing manual coding efforts.
  • Create Forms and Workflows: Quickly develop forms and workflows with AI assistance, accelerating development.
  • Build Robust Mobile Solutions: Leverage AI to minimize manual effort, allowing focus on delivering impactful solutions.

Learn more in this blog by @BirgitS. 

SAPUI5 Development with TypeScript and UI5 Linter

AI-Driven Migration to TypeScript with Joule: Joule, the AI assistant integrated into SAP Build Code, now enables a seamless migration of SAPUI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. TypeScript offers enhanced development efficiency, providing a structured and scalable approach to building SAPUI5 apps. Learn more about this powerful capability in this blog by @martinhaeuser.

Release of UI5 Linter v1.0: SAP Build Code introduces UI5 Linter v1.0, a tool designed to ensure code quality and alignment with best practices. This release empowers developers to write cleaner, more maintainable SAPUI5 code, enhancing the overall development lifecycle.

A New Experience for Extending SAP Fiori Apps

Adaptation Editor

Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 Projects: SAP Build Code now features the Adaptation Editor, a new tool for extending SAPUI5 applications. Replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, the Adaptation Editor is available in SAP Business Application Studio, offering an intuitive and consistent experience across environments. Developers can leverage this updated tool for streamlined SAPUI5 extensions with improved performance. For details, see the announcement blog by @OliverGraeff. 

Adaptation vs. Extension Projects: SAPUI5 flexibility supports both adaptation and extension projects, enabling tailored app modifications for developers, key users, and end users. Adaptation projects, now the strategic approach for extending SAPUI5 apps, provide advanced tools for code-based customization. To learn about the differences and benefits of these project types, explore this comparison blog.

SAP Build Apps

Additional HTTP Request Headers for BTP Destinations

Gain greater control and flexibility when integrating external services with SAP Build Apps. You can now configure additional HTTP request headers for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) destinations, enhancing integration security and customization. The new flow function allows you to upload binary files using multipart/form-data as request body type. To learn more, see HTTP Destination Request flow function reference documentation.

A New Top Navigation Bar

SAP Build Apps now has an updated toolbar and navigation menu for a more intuitive experience. Frequently used features like Variables are now accessible directly from the toolbar. Tabs like "UI Canvas" and "Data" have been renamed to "User Interface" and "Integrations." At the same time, less-used options such as Theme and Navigation are grouped under "App Settings" to declutter the interface. Actions like Preview and Publish are prominently placed in the upper-right corner, simplifying release versioning and deployment. For more details, read the feature review blog post by @Dan_Wroblewski.

SAP Build Work Zone

Integration with Joule for Work Zone Advanced Edition

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities. This integration delivers intelligent workspace recommendations to boost efficiency, proactive insights tailored to tasks and priorities, and streamlined workflows to improve collaboration and focus. For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, check out @Yumnahabbasi's blog post.

Guided Process Wizard

The Guided Process Wizard combines SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation to simplify creating structured, step-by-step workflows. With this integration, you can design guided processes in Process Automation and deploy them as wizards in Work Zone. Build customizable workflows with dynamic UI elements like cards and widgets to streamline complex onboarding and invoice processing tasks. Guided experiences are included in the SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. Read more in the FAQ blogpost by @Yumnahabbasi.

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • What's New Viewer
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Road Map Explorer

To stay updated with the latest announcements and innovations, follow the SAP Builders Community and converse with fellow SAP Builders.

We look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve your goals!
